features.summary: Prologis reported strong Q2 2022 results with core FFO of $1.11 per share, 46% rent change on rollover, and 97.7% occupancy. The company increased full-year earnings guidance to $5.14-$5.18 per share and raised market rent growth forecasts to 23% globally and 25% in the US. Despite macroeconomic headwinds, fundamentals remain robust with 52% of remaining availabilities under proposal versus 38% pre-COVID, and 71% of leases expiring in the next 12 months either preleased or in negotiations.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strength in fundamentals despite some normalization from peak demand levels. They forecast market rent growth to remain elevated due to exceptionally tight vacancy rates and construction cost inflation providing a pricing umbrella. The company anticipates net effective same-store NOI growth to exceed 8% through 2025 based on current lease mark-to-market of 56%, representing over $2 billion of embedded annual NOI growth as leases roll.
features.risks: Key risks include potential supply-demand imbalances, though management disputes market concerns about oversupply in their core markets. Economic uncertainty could delay some customer expansion decisions and supply chain resilience investments. Amazon's reported space rationalization has generated investor concern, though Prologis sees minimal impact in their portfolio with zero spaces currently in play from their 132 Amazon locations.
features.key_drivers: Primary drivers include structural demand from supply chain resilience needs and continued e-commerce adoption running 150 basis points ahead of pre-COVID expectations. Construction cost inflation and extended development timelines are limiting new supply while supporting rent growth. The company benefits from a $2.5 billion land bank providing development optionality and 56% lease mark-to-market creating significant embedded growth through lease rollovers.
